steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The passage says the authors describe how restorative sleep can be for young people. The correct quotation must directly show sleep's restorative power, especially for children.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — “Then let no one turn with too much [fussiness] from the simple story of the humble Elleanor, though it may contain few, or none, of the thrilling charms of poetry and passion.”: says nothing about sleep or its effects on children. ✗
  • Choice B — “Home is home, to the lowly as well as the great; and no rank, or color, destroys its sacred character, its power over the mind, and the affections.”: addresses home, not sleep. ✗
  • Choice C — “Blessed are the slumbers of the innocent! They are kindlier than balm, and they refresh and gladden the spirit of childhood, like ministerings from a better world.”: "Blessed are the slumbers of the innocent! They are kindlier than balm, and they refresh and gladden the spirit of childhood" directly illustrates restorative sleep for young people. ✓
  • Choice D — “And now, dear reader, a word with you. What is done cannot be undone. We cannot unravel this web of iniquity, and extract Justice from this mass of cruel wrongs.”: unrelated to sleep or restoration. ✗
That matches Choice C — “Blessed are the slumbers of the innocent! They are kindlier than balm, and they refresh and gladden the spirit of childhood, like ministerings from a better world.”.
